evals.prompt: Run the e2e judge against the deployed EAGLE app. Just do login, home, and chat journeys. I've set EAGLE_TEST_EMAIL and EAGLE_TEST_PASSWORD in my env already.
evals.expected_output: Orchestrator runs 3 journeys (login, home, chat), captures screenshots including pre-send and 30s interval shots during streaming, judges each with Sonnet via Bedrock, produces results JSON and markdown report with pass/fail verdicts, quality scores, and cost.

evals.prompt: I just deployed a frontend change to the EAGLE app. Can you do a quick visual check of the admin dashboard and chat page to make sure nothing looks broken? Use the deployed URL.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this as an e2e-judge task. Runs the orchestrator with --journeys admin,chat against the internal ALB. Reports pass/fail for each step, highlights any visual issues found by the Sonnet judge, and shows the quality scores.

evals.prompt: Run a full visual regression test on all pages of EAGLE and upload the results to S3 so we can see them in the dashboard.
evals.expected_output: Runs the orchestrator with --journeys all --upload-s3. Captures screenshots across all journeys (login, home, chat, admin, documents, responsive), judges each, uploads results + screenshots to S3, and points user to /admin/e2e-judge dashboard page.

evals.prompt: I want to run the full acquisition package e2e test -- the complete lifecycle from intake through document generation, revision, finalize, and export. Test against the deployed app. EAGLE_TEST_EMAIL and EAGLE_TEST_PASSWORD are set.
evals.expected_output: Runs the orchestrator with --journeys acquisition_package against the internal ALB. Explains this is a multi-turn chat journey covering: intake ($750K cloud hosting), compliance analysis, SOW + IGCE + MR + AP generation, workflows/packages page check with document checklist, SOW v2 revision (Section 508 + FedRAMP), package finalization, and ZIP export. Mentions ~30+ screenshots across all phases, cost estimate, and that it takes longer than single-page journeys due to 7 chat turns.
assertions: Uses --journeys acquisition_package flag
Uses the correct internal ALB URL (internal-eaglec-front-teerfwosqs71)
Does NOT hardcode credentials — uses env vars
Explains the journey covers intake, document generation, checklist, revision, finalize, and export phases
Mentions the document checklist check (navigates to /workflows page)
Mentions document revision (SOW v2 with new requirements)
Mentions package finalization (finalize_package tool or status change)
Mentions package export as ZIP
Mentions approximate screenshot count (25-35+) and that it takes longer than other journeys
Mentions cost estimate

evals.prompt: After generating all documents, I want to verify the document checklist shows the right status and then test the revision + finalize flow. Can the e2e judge handle that?
evals.expected_output: Identifies this as the acquisition_package journey (or subset). Explains the journey navigates to /workflows to check the document checklist with completion status for each document type (SOW, IGCE, MR, AP), then returns to chat to request a document revision (SOW v2), and finally triggers package finalization. Shows the orchestrator command with --journeys acquisition_package.
assertions: Correctly identifies the acquisition_package journey as covering this use case
Explains the document checklist check (shows which docs are completed/pending)
Explains the revision flow (SOW v2 with updated requirements)
Explains the finalize step (validates completeness, changes package status)
Uses the correct internal ALB URL
Does NOT hardcode credentials
Mentions the /workflows page for checklist verification

evals.prompt: Run a full visual regression on everything including the acquisition package lifecycle, and upload to S3. I need to see intake through export tested end to end.
evals.expected_output: Runs the orchestrator with --journeys all --upload-s3 (which now includes acquisition_package). Lists all 7 journeys: login, home, chat, admin, documents, responsive, acquisition_package. Warns that the acquisition_package journey is significantly longer (7 chat turns, 10+ minutes) and more expensive. Points to /admin/e2e-judge dashboard for results.
assertions: Uses --journeys all --upload-s3 flags
Lists acquisition_package as one of the available journeys
Mentions the acquisition_package journey covers the full lifecycle (intake → export)
Uses the correct internal ALB URL
Does NOT hardcode credentials
Warns about longer runtime or higher cost for the acquisition_package journey
Points user to /admin/e2e-judge dashboard for results

evals.prompt: Run the e2e judge on just the documents journey. I want to make sure the document viewer doesn't show any template jargon like {{VARIABLE}} placeholders, and that .content.md sidecar files aren't appearing as separate entries in the document list. EAGLE_TEST_EMAIL and EAGLE_TEST_PASSWORD are set.
evals.expected_output: Runs the orchestrator with --journeys documents against the internal ALB. The enhanced documents journey captures 6-7 screenshots covering the document list (checking for .content.md sidecar entries), document previews (checking for {{PLACEHOLDER}} template jargon), and templates modal. Reports pass/fail with specific template jargon issues identified.
assertions: Uses --journeys documents flag
Uses the correct internal ALB URL (internal-eaglec-front-teerfwosqs71)
Does NOT hardcode credentials — uses env vars
Explains that the documents journey checks for .content.md sidecar files in the document list
Explains that the judge detects {{PLACEHOLDER}} template jargon or unfilled placeholders in document content
Does NOT navigate to /templates (a non-existent route) — uses the Templates button/modal on the documents page instead
Mentions opening document previews to check content for template variables
Mentions cost estimate
